title: senecio lopezii
intro: Senecio lopezii, also known as the "Lopez's ragwort," is a perennial plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and flowers contain sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and providing antioxidant properties. Senecio lopezii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat various health conditions, such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.
